title: RegexpFilter silently swallows invalid runtime regex errors
body: RegexpFilter.Filter calls `regexp.Match(pattern, matchdata)` (a second compile after keyword substitution) and on error just `return false, nil` -- so a `-mr`/`-fr` pattern that becomes invalid after FUZZ keyword substitution just never matches anything, with no indication to the user that their filter is broken.

title: Wordlists are loaded entirely into memory
body: WordlistInput.readFile reads the whole wordlist into a `[][]byte` slice up front. For multi-gigabyte wordlists (common for subdomain/content discovery) this uses a huge amount of RAM before the first request is even sent. Could this stream from the file instead of buffering everything?

title: IgnoreWordlistComments strips '#' mid-word incorrectly
body: stripComments() treats any ` #` (space then hash) later in a line as a comment marker and truncates the word there. Wordlist entries that legitimately contain a URL fragment like `page.php#section` get truncated to `page.php` when they don't even have a leading space before the `#`... but words with a literal space+hash (e.g. copied from a commented export) lose real content silently.

title: Feature parity between Windows and POSIX const files unclear
body: input/const_windows.go and output/const_windows.go define separate constants from their POSIX counterparts (const.go), but there's no changelog or doc noting which ffuf features are degraded or unavailable on Windows as a result.
